snp2.5:2.0.270: “What is the source of lust and hatred?Where are born discontent, delight, and hair-raising terror?Having originated from what do the mind’s thoughtstoss one around as boys toss up a crow?”(1)
snp2.5:2.0.271: “Lust and hatred have their source here;from this are born discontent, delight, and hair-raising terror.Having originated from this, the mind’s thoughtstoss one around as boys toss up a crow. (2) [49]
snp2.5:2.0.272: “Born from affection, arisen from oneself,like the trunk-born shoots of the banyan tree,manifold, attached to sensual pleasures,like a māluvācreeper stretched across the woods. (3)
snp2.5:2.0.273: “Those who understand their source,they dispel it — listen, O yakkha! —they cross this flood so hard to cross,uncrossed before, for no renewed existence.” (4)
